inline_comments.message: Does this work with creating of new resources?

I am wondering whether we should expose two endpoint on K8s API level. POST to explicit use it to create new resource with Configuration API, and PUT to explicit Update resource when already exist. Then we do not have to wonder whether update will create also and moreover we will keep the mechanism more transparent. Also the call'er of the API will need to decide if want to create or update what all is good. With this proposed implementation we may want to update resource, and we can accidentally create a new one instead...

The other options is also to do here internally GET - to check if the resource already exists and to call k8sClient.createResources or k8sClient.updateResources respectively.
shouldn't we have here resource helm.KubernetesResource instead?

When this will not happen and create will be succesful we will just after do update as well. So if create is ok, we shouldn't do update later on. I would also report error only if both create and update will fail - the message of both errors should be printed then. If create will fail and update will succeeed then none of the errors should be printed.
Please squash commit message before merge
Please provide UTs for new `semi-public` function
